name: Brabant Killers
description: The Brabant Killers (also known as the Nijvel Gang or the Crazy Brabant Killers) refers to a series of violent attacks, primarily armed robberies of supermarkets, that occurred in Belgium between 1982 and 1985, resulting in 28 deaths and numerous injuries. The perpetrators remain unidentified despite decades of investigation. Conspiracy theories abound, suggesting involvement ranging from far-right groups and elements within the Belgian Gendarmerie to foreign intelligence agencies. Proponents of these theories cite the seemingly excessive violence used during the robberies, the lack of clear financial gain (often the loot was small), and alleged cover-ups or incompetence during the investigations. They also point to inconsistencies in witness testimonies and potential links between the attacks and political instability in Belgium at the time. Mainstream perspectives acknowledge the failures of the investigation but generally attribute the events to a combination of factors, including police incompetence, the chaotic nature of the crimes, and the difficulty of identifying perpetrators after such a long period. While acknowledging the possibility of organized crime involvement, they tend to dismiss more elaborate conspiracy theories as lacking concrete evidence and fueled by public frustration with the unresolved case. The official investigation has been plagued by errors and accusations of deliberate obstruction, further feeding the conspiracy narratives. The case continues to be a source of national trauma and speculation in Belgium.
subtitle: Unsolved Belgian supermarket massacres.

claims.claim: The Brabant Killers were part of a Gladio-style stay-behind network.
claims.evidence: Links to far-right groups and alleged connections to former Gendarmerie officers.
claims.criticism: No direct evidence linking the killers to Gladio. Allegations rely on circumstantial evidence and speculation.

claims.claim: The attacks were politically motivated, intended to destabilize Belgium.
claims.evidence: The timing of the attacks coincided with political turmoil and economic recession.
claims.criticism: Difficult to prove a direct causal link between the attacks and political goals. Economic hardship and political instability are common during that period.

claims.claim: The investigation was deliberately obstructed to protect those involved.
claims.evidence: Numerous errors and inconsistencies in the investigation, missing evidence, and alleged cover-ups.
claims.criticism: The investigation was undeniably flawed, but attribution to deliberate obstruction is difficult to prove. Could also be attributed to incompetence and poor investigative practices.

keyFigures.name: Christiaan Bonkoffsky
keyFigures.role: Former Gendarmerie officer implicated in conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Martine Gozlan
keyFigures.role: Journalist who investigated the case and wrote about potential conspiracies.

keyFigures.name: Frederik François
keyFigures.role: Judge who has been involved in the investigation and has acknowledged its shortcomings.

mythologyConnections: The idea of a hidden hand manipulating events.
The archetype of the masked killer.

timeline.event: Series of attacks attributed to the Brabant Killers.
Last known attack attributed to the Brabant Killers.
Ongoing investigation and speculation about the identity of the perpetrators.
